title: Podman-remote run should wait for exit code
body:
This change matches what is happening on the podman local side
and should eliminate a race condition.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Hopefully fixes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3870
@mheon @baude @edsantiago PTAL
Code LGTM

@edsantiago has a tests that was revealing this error.

> LGTM, any chance we can test that?

Testing negatives/error-cases of a race is very difficult to do reliably in automation.  I can manually run the change through the reproducer I used before, however the results cannot be conclusive - since code-change nearly always causes a timing change :confused:

> Cirrus-CI (internally) us suppose to decrypt those [ENCRYPTED[... strings,

I think that's a red herring. My suspicion is that the problem is elsewhere, but Cirrus is showing `ENCRYPTED` because, um, it probably shouldn't show the real value in a public log

> ENCRYPTED because, um, it probably shouldn't show the real value in a public log

It mangles the output with `SECRET` for encrypted values.

@rhatdan There's some problem here, when I run Ed's reproducer using code from this PR on Fedora 30:

```
...cut...
STEP 4: COMMIT build_test
549471035c7a6927e8dc2c52e2a87456327bc0d1b561869c90ea5d0c1bd79289
+ pr run --rm build_test cat /myfile
+ podman-remote run --rm build_test cat /myfile
hi
Error: Virtual Read failed, 0: EOF
[root@cevich-fedora-29-libpod-5420341990522880 libpod]# 
```

On Fedora 30, it simply hangs after printing 'hi' and never returns (I waited 5-minutes).  I'll look at the Cirrus-CI logs next...
...ya, similar story in the integration-tests: ```Timed out after 90.000s.``` across the board, for all remote-client tests except cgroupv2.

Ref: VM Images I'm using are:

`fedora-30-libpod-5420341990522880`
`fedora-29-libpod-5420341990522880`
(the same that were failing in #3870) using reproducer that Ed wrote:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -xe

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d --tmpdir podman-remote-test.XXXXXX)
cat >$tmpdir/Dockerfile <<EOF
FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest
RUN apk add nginx
RUN echo hi >/myfile
EOF

pr() { podman-remote "$@"; }

while :;do
    pr build -t build_test --format=docker $tmpdir
    pr run --rm build_test cat /myfile
    pr rmi -f build_test
done
```

like this from the repository root:

```bash
make podman podman-remote
make install PREFIX=/usr
systemctl enable io.podman.socket
systemctl enable io.podman.service
systemctl restart io.podman.socket
systemctl restart io.podman.service
chmod +x repro.sh
./repro.sh
...cut...
```

@cevich @edsantiago Total rewrite of original patch, turns out the error, I believe, was on the server side.  We were not waiting for a full exit, so I think the client side could exit before the container was cleaned up.

While in this code, I figured out why container exit codes were not being propagated.
@baude @mheon @giuseppe @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
LGTM assuming happy tests.
LGTM but I'd like a nod from @baude pre-merge
System tests are failing for most remote client jobs. CGroups v2 remote succeeded, though?
/retest lint
spinning up the 3870 reproducer with this latest code...

@cevich @mheon has a fix that might also fix the shm issue.  We were umounting /dev/shm as MNT_DETATCH, his latest fix, has it wait to be unmounted.
ahh, I see the shm in the path, ok.

Updated all packages, rebooted, rebuilt/installed, re-ran.  The VM is `104.198.147.244` if it would be easier to to poke at it directly.  `$GOSRC` is `/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod` and script is `~/repro.sh` (make sure $GOSRC is set).
Removal can happen via a separate process, but we do need to force a remove in attached `run --rm` to ensure the container is gone by the time `podman run` exits.
Probably unrelated to `conmon` - this is specifically Podman unconditionally forcing a remove before exit on `podman run --rm` calls

@cevich there's one possibility that I've been worrying about: are these test VMs long-running, or ad-hoc per CI job? If they're long-running, there's a possibility that podman-varlink will start for one CI job, then something goes wrong and leaves that podman-varlink process running forever, so subsequent podman-remote commands contact an old varlink process. Is that in any way possible?

> subsequent podman-remote commands contact an old varlink process. Is that in any way possible?

@edsantiago it's good out-of-the-box thinking and thanks for sharing.  We get new, freshly booted VMs for every task (including re-runs).  The only way the environment could become polluted as you describe, is if that state is set in the cache-image **and** enabled/started on boot.

While we **do** install the podman package to ensure all dependencies are brought in.  So, what you describe would be possible if the `io.podman.service` (and socket) is enabled on boot and therefor  resident before the files are removed in preparation for source build/install.

This is something we can check for (I'll open a PR).  When testing @rhatdan patches here, I was careful to search down and remove all the podman and podman-remote binaries before rebuilding/reinstalling.  I also was careful to do a `systemd daemon-reload` and restart the `io.podman` units.
Opened #3985
rebasing and re-testing this since master moved a few times...

Running from pure upstream/master (c1761ba1a) I get:

```
Error: error removing containers [eec0343cbaf9cafe616a17e71eaefc2c27ed62cf441b5c2f2f23f5581ec6a40f] for image "cad64afab665c2071042eb3298acb85d95cd9c44a98fbf8ba5148577084be123": could not remove container "eec0343cbaf9cafe616a17e71eaefc2c27ed62cf441b5c2f2f23f5581ec6a40f": unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/eec0343cbaf9cafe616a17e71eaefc2c27ed62cf441b5c2f2f23f5581ec6a40f/userdata/shm: device or resource busy
```

New reproducer script:
(before running, do `cp /usr/libexec/podman/conmon /usr/bin`)

```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -ex

for type in service socket
do
	systemctl disable io.podman.$type || :
	systemctl stop io.podman.$type || :
done
cd /
find /usr -type f -name 'podman*' -exec rm -f '{}' +
cd /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod
rm /usr/bin/podman* /usr/local/bin/podman* || true
rm -rf /var/lib/containers
systemctl daemon-reload

make clean
make podman podman-remote
make install
make install PREFIX=/usr

sync && echo 3 > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart io.podman.service
systemctl restart io.podman.socket

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d --tmpdir podman-remote-test.XXXXXX)
cat >$tmpdir/Dockerfile <<EOF
FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest
RUN apk add nginx
RUN echo hi >/myfile
EOF

pr() { podman-remote "$@"; }

while :;do
    pr build -t build_test --format=docker $tmpdir
    pr run --rm build_test cat /myfile
    pr rmi -f build_test
done
```
The major difference between reproducing above, and previously is the `dnf update -y && reboot` I ran.  I'll get a fresh VM and try again, once Matt's done looking at this one.

Rebased onto #3985 just to be safe, spun up a VM w/o updating it's packages.  Running [the reproducer  above](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3934#issuecomment-530062589) I'm still getting `Error: Virtual Read failed, 0: EOF` when the script does `podman-remote run --rm build_test cat /myfile`.

Perhaps interestingly, this is also some kind of race, because when I change to `podman-remote run -it --rm build_test cat /myfile` it ***sometimes*** works fine for 3-5 iterations before bombing.  I also tried rebasing against master (093013b099cd2397fdf3aedc213d182064be5d27) but the behavior remains :confounded: 

```
...cut...
STEP 3: RUN echo hi >/myfile
STEP 4: COMMIT build_test

+ podman-remote run -it --rm build_test cat /myfile
```

@baude Matt took a look at a VM behaving this way, and concluded:

> i have no clue what's going on there. early varlink hangup probably

would you mind giving it a go (rebase this PR against master to be safe) when you have a chance?

(note: current CI results here are not helpful until rhatdan rebases the PR)

Okay, the `EOF` issue is partially addressed with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3998 and I was successfully able to confirm the fix in this PR does appear to fix the #3870 problem.  So let's get this in.

LGTM
[This is a new error in a start test I haven't seen before](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5760809608675328?command=integration_test#L6994) though it's Ubuntu, so maybe a flake?
uggg, sorry, I shouldn't tie up Dan's PR with more problems, let me open an issue for that race...
...opened https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4021
(re-ran flaked test)
After this PR, we should have a guarantee that the container is gone when the original Podman process exits.

Nevermind. We don't have an explicit remove in https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/82ac0d8925dbb5aa738f1494ecb002eb6daca992/pkg/adapter/containers_remote.go#L462

We probably need to add one

@mheon the remove happens on the server side in the adapter code.
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3934/files#diff-d6a6befd6e8af99104d67db5ea6786faR106

I think this is ready to merge.

@mheon @giuseppe @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @cevich @edsantiago PTAL

Are we guaranteed that the client doesn't exit until the server is done removing, though?
LGTM regardless
/lgtm

> Are we guaranteed that the client doesn't exit until the server is done removing, though?

Possibly not, that's why I opened #4021
I believe that we are guaranteed for the removal to happen before the front end gets signaled.
